meta.title: MCC-UG Information Bulletin & Counselling Scheme
meta.documentNote: Content sourced from the official MCC NEET-UG (MBBS/BDS/B.Sc. Nursing) Information Bulletin. Verify schedule and seat matrix on mcc.nic.in before registration.

candidateGuidelines: Qualified and eligible candidates must register on www.mcc.nic.in to participate in counselling. MCC does not allot seats on nomination basis or manually.
All questions in the counselling scheme are mandatory. Read the scheme before registering.
Completing online registration constitutes acceptance of the counselling scheme and terms & conditions.
Applications are accepted only online via mcc.nic.in — no other mode is valid.
Fill the application yourself; do not use agents or third parties.
Only one registration per candidate. Duplicate registrations lead to debarment and cancellation.
Security deposit is forfeited if a Round 2 (or later) allotted seat is not joined, or if admission is cancelled for wrong information or missing documents.
Registration does not guarantee a seat — admission depends on merit, eligibility, and institutional criteria.
Information entered at registration is treated as final; MCC does not modify name, DoB, category, nationality, or contact details.
NTA registration data is pre-populated for counselling. NEET-UG eligibility requires 50% in PCB (40% for SC/ST/OBC-NCL/PwBD as per NMC norms) and a valid NEET rank.
Stipend, fee structure, bond, and rural service conditions are provided by colleges — verify directly before locking choices.
Confirm domicile/eligibility for Central University institutional quota seats before opting.
Confirm fee structure from colleges, especially Deemed Universities, before choice filling.
Stay updated via mcc.nic.in for schedules, results, and notices — MCC does not contact candidates individually.
MCC reserves the right to alter instructions in the information bulletin; DGHS interpretation is final.
Use the same mobile number and email registered with NTA — OTPs are sent only to those.
Court cases related to counselling must be in Delhi jurisdiction.
Central Pool nomination seats are handled by other ministries — not by MCC.
All admissions and resignations (where permitted) are online only; download allotment letters from MCC.
Security deposit refunds are credited to the same account used for payment.

introduction.title: NEET UG Counselling
introduction.summary: MCC of DGHS conducts online counselling for MBBS/BDS seat allotment based on NEET-UG ranks, as approved by the Hon'ble Supreme Court.
points: Under the Modified Scheme (Sharwan Kumar vs DGHS, 2012), MCC conducts online counselling for 15% All India Quota seats in participating government medical/dental colleges.
Following Dar-Us-Slam Educational Trust vs MCI (2017), MCC also counsels 100% seats in participating Deemed Universities.

DGHS is the designated authority for 15% AIQ of contributing states, all central government MBBS seats, universities established by Act of Parliament, and Deemed Universities (MCI Gazette, 27 June 2017).
AIQ counselling runs four rounds: Round 1, Round 2, Round 3 (formerly Mop-up), and Stray Vacancy Round (Nihila P.P. vs MCC, 2021). No seats revert to states after Round 2.
Upgradation is permitted up to Round 3. Candidates joining Round 3 cannot resign from AIQ/Deemed/Central Universities or participate in further counselling.
Centre–State data sharing eliminates candidates already allotted in Round 3 from the All India Stray Vacancy Round.
MCC software ensures uniform counselling patterns across states (Supreme Court, Contempt Petition 289/2022, from AY 2022–23).
Foreign national seat criteria follow Supreme Court judgments in Anushka Rengunthwar vs Union of India and related Delhi High Court orders.
Implementation difficulties may be brought before the Supreme Court (Sharwan Kumar vs DGHS, 1992).

roleOfMcc.title: Role of MCC in NEET
roleOfMcc.summary: MCC/DGHS counsels 15% AIQ, 100% Deemed Universities, Central Universities (DU, AMU, BHU including institutional/domicile quota), ESIC, AFMC (registration only), IP University colleges (VMMC & SJH, ABVIMS & RML, ESIC Dental), 100% AIIMS, 100% JIPMER, and B.Sc. Nursing (central institutes).

scope.institutions: Government medical/dental colleges of participating states
ESIC medical colleges — IP quota seats
Registration on MCC; counselling by AFMC authorities
VMMC & SJH, ABVIMS & RML, ESIC Dental (15% AIQ + 85% institutional)
Puducherry & Karaikal — open and state quota seats

roleOfMcc.limitation: MCC's role is limited to seat allotment based on merit and choices, using candidate data received from NTA after NEET-UG.

ociParity.judgment: Anushka Rengunthwar & Ors. vs Union of India & Ors. (03.02.2023)
ociParity.summary: OCI cardholders are treated at par with Indian citizens for UR (General) and NRI seats in MCC counselling. OCI secured after 04.03.2021 is governed prospectively; similarly placed OCI holders retain earlier rights.

rounds.name: Round 1
rounds.description: Initial online choice filling and seat allotment.

rounds.name: Round 2
rounds.description: Second round with fresh registration and choice filling for remaining seats.

rounds.name: Round 3
rounds.description: Formerly Mop-up round. Seat conversion applies. Joining is mandatory — no resignation permitted.

rounds.name: Stray Vacancy Round
rounds.description: Online round for remaining vacant seats after Round 3.

aiq.title: 15% All India Quota
aiq.summary: Four rounds of online AIQ counselling for NEET-UG qualified candidates. Domicile-free access to 15% seats in state government colleges and listed open pools.
openSeatsDomicileFree: 15% All India Quota MBBS/BDS seats of states
AIIMS open seats — 100% MBBS across India
15% AIQ of DU / IP University (VMMC, ABVIMS, ESIC Dental)
Jamia open seats — Faculty of Dentistry
eligibility: Candidate must have qualified NEET UG.
Domicile free.
Must meet NMC norms.
Only candidates qualified/eligible for AIQ undergraduate seats participate in MCC allotment.
aiq.conversionNote: Seat conversion is carried out during Round 3 when eligible candidates in the conversion category are exhausted.
aiq.roster: 15% AIQ roster applied by MCC/DGHS as per DoPT, MoHFW norms.
aiq.stateQuotaNote: For state quota and private colleges, contact the respective state government/admission authority — MCC cannot guide on state admissions.

deemed.title: 100% Deemed University
deemed.summary: MCC conducts four online rounds for 100% Deemed University seats per Supreme Court directions (Dar-Us-Slam, 12.12.2022).

reservationPolicy.note: No reservation for OBC/SC/ST/PwD/EWS in Deemed Universities.
reservationPolicy.minorityNote: Certain Deemed universities may have Jain or Muslim minority reservation — verify eligibility before applying.

eligibility: Candidate must have qualified NEET UG.
Domicile free.
All India Quota qualified candidates including J&K UT are eligible.
deemed.conversionNote: During Round 3, unutilized Jain/Muslim/NRI seats convert to Private Deemed UR seats after category exhaustion.

aiims.title: All AIIMS Across the Country
aiims.summary: Four MCC rounds for 100% AIIMS MBBS seats. Central reservation applies to seats contributed to MCC.
aiims.foreignNationalNote: Foreign National seats — only OCI/PIO/Foreign Nationals eligible. Fees determined by AIIMS authorities.
eligibility: Candidate must have qualified NEET UG.
Domicile free.
AIIMS-specific eligibility conditions apply.
aiims.nominationNote: MCC does not allot seats through nomination basis.

esic.title: ESIC — Insured Persons Quota
esic.summary: Insured Persons Quota seats from state quota are domicile-free. MCC conducts four online rounds per ESIC norms.
eligibility: Ward of Insured Persons certificate with distinct number; eligible list provided by ESIC.
Qualified NEET UG for the relevant year.
Domicile free.
Reservation as per Government of India guidelines.
esic.roster: IP quota roster applied by MCC/DGHS.
esic.nominationNote: MCC does not allot seats through nomination basis.
esic.advisory: Verify eligibility with ESIC well in advance before applying for IP Quota.

centralUniversities.title: Central University / Institute
centralUniversities.summary: MCC counsels 15% AIQ and 85% institutional/state quota for central institutes. Four online rounds.
disclaimers: 85% institutional quota eligibility is set by colleges/institutes — MCC does not determine it.
Verify eligibility before applying; wrong information leads to cancellation and security deposit forfeiture.
Round 3 seat conversion applies when category candidates are exhausted.

institutes.name: Delhi University
institutes.institutionalRule: Candidates who studied 11th & 12th in Delhi NCT are eligible for 85% state quota.
institutes.cwNote: CW reservation for state quota only — verify eligibility with DU.
institutes.roster: Roster on 100% seats (15% AIQ + 85% state quota) applied by MCC.

institutes.name: Jamia Millia Islamia — Faculty of Dentistry
institutes.institutionalRule: Jamia has its own reservation policy and internal candidate eligibility — refer to Jamia Information Bulletin.
institutes.roster: MCC allots per roster/seat matrix provided by JMI.

institutes.name: VMMC & SJH, ABVIMS & RML, ESIC Dental
institutes.institutionalRule: Candidates who studied 10+2 (11th & 12th) in recognized schools/colleges in NCT of Delhi and passed qualifying exam from NCT of Delhi qualify for state quota.
institutes.cwNote: 5% horizontal CW reservation for state quota — verify with college.
institutes.roster: Roster on 15% AIQ and 85% internal quota applied by MCC.

amu.title: Aligarh Muslim University
amu.summary: Four MCC rounds for AMU seats. Open seats have no reservation except 5% horizontal PwD.

reservationPolicy.openSeats: No reservation on AMU open seats except 5% PwD horizontal reservation.
reservationPolicy.internalQuota: All internal quota seats are UR — reserved for AMU internal candidates per AMU eligibility.

amu.internalProcess: MCC provides registered internal quota candidates; AMU verifies and returns eligible list to MCC.
eligibility: Check AMU internal quota criteria on AMU website (Annexure 9).
MCC does not decide internal eligibility criteria.
amu.roster: As per seat matrix from AMU authorities.
amu.nominationNote: MCC does not allot seats through nomination basis.

bhu.title: Banaras Hindu University
bhu.summary: All NEET-UG qualified candidates across India may participate in IMS BHU and IDS BHU counselling via MCC.
eligibility: Domicile free.
NEET-UG qualified candidates eligible.
bhu.roster: MCC applies roster on 100% seats contributed by BHU for medical and dental courses.
bhu.nominationNote: MCC does not allot seats through nomination basis.

afmc.title: Armed Forces Medical College (AFMC)
afmc.summary: MCC role is limited to registration of willing candidates. AFMC conducts its own counselling.
process: Register on MCC portal (www.mcc.nic.in) to express interest.
Registered candidate list is sent to AFMC for counselling per AFMC rules.
MCC is not responsible for AFMC allotment decisions.
Verify medical and general eligibility on AFMC website before applying.
MCC will not entertain queries regarding AFMC counselling.
afmc.advisory: Admissions have been cancelled for non-fulfillment of medical eligibility — verify conditions well in advance.

jipmer.title: JIPMER — Puducherry & Karaikal
jipmer.summary: Four MCC rounds for JIPMER seats. JIPMER defines eligibility for state quota; domicile list provided by DME Puducherry.
jipmer.reservationNote: Central reservation applies only on open seats contributed by JIPMER.
eligibility: Candidate must have qualified NEET UG.
Refer Annexure 10 for JIPMER quota eligibility (provided by JIPMER — MCC has no role).
Domicile list for internal seats provided by Puducherry state authorities.
jipmer.roster: MCC applies roster on seats provided by JIPMER.
jipmer.advisory: Review JIPMER Information Bulletin for eligibility and seat status before applying.
